line: All that you touch and all that you see is all your life will ever be.
And everything under the sun is in tune, but the sun is eclipsed by the moon.
The sun is the same in a relative way but you're older, shorter of breath and one day closer to death.
Ticking away the moments that make up a dull day.
The child is grown, the dream is gone.
Hello? Is there anybody in there? Just nod if you can hear me.
There is no pain you are receding, a distant ship's smoke on the horizon.
How I wish, how I wish you were here.
We're just two lost souls swimming in a fish bowl, year after year.
Money, it's a gas. Grab that cash with both hands and make a stash.
Money, so they say, is the root of all evil today.
Run, rabbit run. Dig that hole, forget the sun.
Long you live and high you fly, and smiles you'll give and tears you'll cry.
Remember when you were young, you shone like the sun.
Come on you target for faraway laughter, come on you stranger, you legend, you martyr, and shine!

song: Another Brick in the Wall Pt. 2 [1]
line: We don't need no education. We don't need no thought control.

song: Another Brick in the Wall Pt. 2 [2]
line: All in all it's just another brick in the wall.
The grass was greener, the light was brighter.
Looking beyond the embers of bridges glowing behind us.
Together we stand, divided we fall.
Hey you, out there in the cold, getting lonely, getting old, can you feel me?
Strangers passing in the street, by chance two separate glances meet.
And I am you and what I see is me.
You have to be trusted by the people that you lie to.
And when you lose control, you'll reap the harvest you have sown.
The lunatic is on the grass.
And if the band you're in starts playing different tunes, I'll see you on the dark side of the moon.
And after all, we're only ordinary men.
Forward he cried from the rear, and the front rank died.

song: The Great Gig in the Sky [1]
line: And I am not frightened of dying. Any time will do, I don't mind.

song: The Great Gig in the Sky [2]
line: Why should I be frightened of dying? There's no reason for it, you've gotta go sometime.
Mother, do you think they'll drop the bomb?
Mother, should I trust the government?
Big man, pig man. Ha, ha, charade you are.
You well heeled big wheel, ha, ha, charade you are.
Harmlessly passing your time in the grassland away.
What do you get for pretending the danger's not real.
Tongue-tied and twisted, just an earth-bound misfit, I.
A soul in tension that's learning to fly.
I knew the waiting had begun.
Where were you when I was burned and broken?
Welcome my son, welcome to the machine.
What did you dream? It's alright we told you what to dream.
Fearlessly the idiot faced the crowd, smiling.
And as you rise above the treeline and the clouds.
Come in here, dear boy, have a cigar. You're gonna go far.
And did we tell you the name of the game, boy? We call it riding the gravy train.
By the way, which one's Pink?
Did you see the frightened ones? Did you hear the falling bombs?
The flames are all long gone, but the pain lingers on.
So ya thought ya might like to go to the show.
If you wanna find out what's behind these cold eyes, you'll just have to claw your way through this disguise.
You better run all day and run all night.
With your empty smile and your hungry heart.
Good morning, Worm your honor. The crown will plainly show the prisoner who now stands before you.
Tear down the wall!
Lime and limpid green, a second scene.
Jupiter and Saturn, Oberon, Miranda and Titania.
Emily tries but misunderstands, ah ooh.
There is no other day. Let's try it another way.

song: Set the Controls for the Heart of the Sun [1]
line: Little by little the night turns around.

song: Set the Controls for the Heart of the Sun [2]
line: Witness the man who raves at the wall, making the shape of his questions to heaven.
On the turning away from the pale and downtrodden.
No more turning away from the weak and the weary.
For millions of years mankind lived just like the animals.
All we need to do is make sure we keep talking.
I was spending my time in the doldrums.
So I open my door to my enemies, and I ask could we wipe the slate clean?
The sweet smell of a great sorrow lies over the land.
One world, one soul, time pass, the river rolls.
Arnold Layne had a strange hobby.
Oh, Arnold Layne, it's not the same, takes two to know, two to know, two to know.
When the fat old sun in the sky is falling.
Summer evening birds are calling.
You shout in your sleep. Perhaps the price is just too steep.
Who are you and who am I to say we know the reason why?
I've got a little black book with my poems in.
I've got thirteen channels of shit on the T.V. to choose from.
Does anybody here remember Vera Lynn?
Remember how she said that we would meet again, some sunny day?
In my rear view mirror the sun is going down.
And as the windshield melts and my tears evaporate, leaving only charcoal to defend.
We bitch and we fight, diss each other on sight.
But this thing that we do, these times spent together.
